function.name: APP_BLOCK
function.description: phone app block native iOS-Family-Controls Android-Usage-Access: block(apps,duration) unblock status

subaction.description: One of: block, unblock, status.

intent.description: Free-form description of which apps to block and for how long; used by the block subaction when explicit selection is missing.

packageNames.description: Android package names to block, e.g. ['com.twitter.android']. Used by block on Android.

appTokens.description: iPhone app tokens from a previous selectApps() call. Used by block on iOS.

durationMinutes.description: How long to block the apps, in minutes. Omit for indefinite block.

function.name: AUTOFILL
function.description: browser autofill: fill(field,domain) whitelist-add(domain,confirm) whitelist-list; allowlist-gated browser-feature-gated

subaction.description: One of: fill, whitelist_add, whitelist_list.

field.description: For fill: one of email, password, name, phone, custom. Tells the password manager which field to resolve.

domain.description: Domain to act on. For fill, used as the tab URL when url is omitted. For whitelist_add, the domain to add.

url.description: Optional explicit tab URL for fill (used for whitelist enforcement).

confirmed.description: For whitelist_add: must be explicitly true. Required to ensure the user approved the addition, not the agent.

function.name: BOOK_TRAVEL
function.description: approval-gated real travel booking flights/hotels missing-detail collection draft-confirm calendar-sync after-approval

offerId.description: Duffel offer id to book if a concrete offer was already chosen.

origin.description: Origin IATA airport code when searching for a flight.

destination.description: Destination IATA airport code when searching for a flight.

returnDate.description: Optional return date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

passengers.description: Passenger details required to create the booking after approval.

calendarSync.description: Optional calendar sync metadata for the booked itinerary event.

function.name: CALENDAR
function.description: calendar+availability+prefs: feed next-event search create update delete trip-window bulk-reschedule propose-times check-availability update-prefs

subaction.description: Which calendar operation to run. Google Calendar: feed, next_event, search_events, create_event, update_event, delete_event, trip_window, bulk_reschedule. Availability: check_availability, propose_times. Preferences: update_preferences.

intent.description: Natural-language description of the calendar request (e.g. "what is on my calendar today", "do i have any flights this week", "create a meeting tomorrow at 3pm").

title.description: Event title when creating a calendar event.

query.description: Search phrase for search_events / travel_itinerary (e.g. flight, dentist, Denver).

queries.description: Optional array of search phrases for search_events. Combined and deduped.

details.description: Structured calendar fields — time bounds, timezone, calendar id, create-event timing, location, and attendees.

durationMinutes.description: Meeting length in minutes. Used by propose_times.

daysAhead.description: Days ahead for propose_times search window (defaults to 7, ignored when windowStart/windowEnd are supplied).

slotCount.description: Number of candidate slots for propose_times (defaults to 3).

windowStart.description: ISO-8601 earliest start of the propose_times search window.

windowEnd.description: ISO-8601 latest end of the propose_times search window.

startAt.description: ISO-8601 start time. Used by check_availability.

endAt.description: ISO-8601 end time. Used by check_availability.

timeZone.description: IANA time zone for update_preferences (interprets preferred hours).

preferredStartLocal.description: Earliest preferred meeting start time-of-day (local HH:MM, 24h).

preferredEndLocal.description: Latest preferred meeting end time-of-day (local HH:MM, 24h).

travelBufferMinutes.description: Minutes to reserve before/after each meeting (0–240).

blackoutWindows.description: Array of { label, startLocal (HH:MM), endLocal (HH:MM), daysOfWeek? (0=Sun..6=Sat) }.

function.name: CALENDLY
function.description: Work with Calendly specifically (calendly.com / api.calendly.com): list event types, check availability against a Calendly event type URI, list Calendly-scheduled events, generate Calendly single-use booking links. Subactions: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link. Use this — NOT the Google-Calendar handler — whenever the user mentions Calendly by name or passes a calendly.com / api.calendly.com URL. Calendly is a separate third-party scheduling product with its own API, event-type URIs, and booking-link flow.

subaction.description: One of: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link. Strongly preferred — when omitted, the handler runs an LLM extraction over the conversation to recover it.

intent.description: Optional free-form description of the user's intent.

eventTypeUri.description: Calendly event type URI. Required for availability and single_use_link.

startDate.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries (availability, upcoming_events).

function.name: CONNECTOR
function.description: connector lifecycle+verify-probes (registry-driven): connect disconnect verify status list

connector.description: Which connector to manage (kind from ConnectorRegistry, e.g. google, x, telegram, signal, discord, imessage, whatsapp, twilio, calendly, duffel, health, browser_bridge). Optional when subaction=list.

subaction.description: Lifecycle operation. connect (start auth/pairing); disconnect (revoke + clear grant); verify (active read/send probe where available). status/list are read-only diagnostics for explicit troubleshooting; prefer provider/core registry context when available. Strongly preferred — when omitted, the handler runs an LLM extraction over the conversation to recover it.

mode.description: Connection mode: local | cloud_managed | remote. Defaults vary by connector.

recentLimit.description: verify only — how many recent messages/dialogs to read where the connector supports passive reads.

query.description: Discord verify only — optional search text to prove browser-message reads.

sendTarget.description: verify only — destination chat/recipient/channel for the self-test send.

sendMessage.description: verify only — text body for the self-test send.

profileId.description: browser_bridge connect only — profile identifier within the browser.

profileLabel.description: browser_bridge connect only — human-readable profile label.

redirectUrl.description: google/x connect only — optional OAuth redirect URL override.

connector.description: Which connector to manage (kind from ConnectorRegistry, e.g. google, x, telegram, signal, discord, imessage, whatsapp, twilio, calendly, duffel, health, browser_bridge). Optional when subaction=list.

subaction.description: Lifecycle operation. connect (start auth/pairing); disconnect (revoke + clear grant); verify (active read/send probe where available). status/list are read-only diagnostics for explicit troubleshooting; prefer provider/core registry context when available. Strongly preferred — when omitted, the handler runs an LLM extraction over the conversation to recover it.

mode.description: Connection mode: local | cloud_managed | remote. Defaults vary by connector.

recentLimit.description: verify only — how many recent messages/dialogs to read where the connector supports passive reads.

query.description: Discord verify only — optional search text to prove browser-message reads.

sendTarget.description: verify only — destination chat/recipient/channel for the self-test send.

sendMessage.description: verify only — text body for the self-test send.

profileId.description: browser_bridge connect only — profile identifier within the browser.

profileLabel.description: browser_bridge connect only — human-readable profile label.

redirectUrl.description: google/x connect only — optional OAuth redirect URL override.

function.name: DEVICE_INTENT
function.description: broadcast device intent/reminder: target all|mobile|desktop|specific title body kind routine_reminder|user_action_requested

subaction.description: Only supported subaction: broadcast.

kind.description: Intent kind: user_action_requested, routine_reminder, attention_request, or state_sync.

target.description: Target device group: all, mobile, desktop, or specific.

title.description: Short notification title.

function.name: ENTITY
function.description: ENTITY = people/relationships. subactions add list log_interaction set_identity set_relationship merge; follow-up cadence belongs to SCHEDULED_TASK; one-off dated call/text reminders belong to LIFE

subaction.description: Which ENTITY operation to run: add, list, log_interaction, set_identity, set_relationship, merge. Follow-up cadence belongs to SCHEDULED_TASK.

intent.description: Free-form user intent used to infer subaction when not set.

name.description: Contact display name. When relationshipId is omitted, the handler resolves an existing contact by this name.

channel.description: Primary channel for the contact (email, telegram, discord, signal, sms, twilio_voice, imessage, whatsapp).

handle.description: Primary handle/address on the chosen channel.

email.description: Optional email address for the contact.

phone.description: Optional phone number for the contact.

reason.description: Optional reason note.

entityId.description: Target entity id. Used by set_identity (force a new identity onto a known entity), merge (target id), and any operation that needs a stable EntityStore id.

platform.description: Identity platform for set_identity (e.g. telegram, slack, email, twitter). Combine with handle.

displayName.description: Display name shown alongside an observed identity (set_identity).

fromEntityId.description: Source entity id for set_relationship. Defaults to 'self' when omitted.

relationshipType.description: Edge type label for set_relationship (e.g. manages, colleague_of, works_at).

sourceEntityIds.description: Entity ids being folded into the target entity (merge). Provide as a JSON array of strings.

evidence.description: Free-form evidence string captured alongside set_identity / set_relationship observations.

function.name: FIRST_RUN
function.description: owner first-run: defaults|customize|replay; defaults asks wake time once

partialAnswers.description: Optional object carrying answers from prior turns (resume support).

function.name: HEALTH
function.description: health/fitness telemetry HealthKit/GoogleFit/Strava/Fitbit/Withings/Oura: today | trend(days) | by_metric(steps heart-rate sleep calories distance workouts) | status

subaction.description: Which health query to run: today, trend, by_metric, status.

intent.description: free-form intent infer subaction

metric.description: Metric for by_metric queries: steps, active_minutes, sleep_hours, heart_rate, calories, distance_meters.

days.description: Window size for trend and by_metric queries.

function.name: LIFE
function.description: life:subaction=create|update|delete(kind=definition|goal) + complete|skip|snooze occurrence + review goal

subaction.description: Which life operation to perform. Owner-policy verbs (policy_set_reminder, policy_configure_escalation) write to OwnerFactStore.

kind.description: For create/update/delete: 'definition' (habit/routine/reminder/alarm/todo) or 'goal' (long-term aspiration). Ignored for occurrence-level verbs (complete/skip/snooze) and review.

intent.description: Natural language description of what to do. Examples: "create a daily brushing habit for morning and night", "snooze brushing for 30 minutes".

title.description: Name for a new item, or the name of an existing item to act on.

target.description: Name or ID of an existing item to act on.

minutes.description: Snooze duration in minutes (alternative to a preset).

details.description: Structured data when needed. May include: cadence schedule record, kind (task/habit/routine), description, priority, progressionRule, reminderPlan, confirmed (boolean when the user explicitly approves a previewed create), preset (snooze preset like 15m/30m/1h/tonight/tomorrow_morning), occurrenceId (target an existing occurrence directly for snooze/complete reminder mutations), goalId, goalTitle, supportStrategy, successCriteria, note, limit, domain (user_lifeops/agent_ops).

function.name: LIFEOPS
function.description: owner LIFEOPS verb: pause|resume|wipe; wipe requires confirmed:true

startIso.description: ISO-8601 start. Defaults to now when verb=pause and unset.

endIso.description: ISO-8601 end (optional — open-ended pause if absent).

reason.description: Optional reason ('vacation', 'sick', etc).

confirmed.description: Required true for verb=wipe. Without it, wipe returns a confirmation prompt.

confirmation.description: Alternative confirmation token — must equal 'wipe' for verb=wipe.

function.name: MESSAGE
function.description: primary message action ops send read_channel read_with_contact search list_channels list_servers join leave react edit delete pin get_user triage list_inbox search_inbox draft_reply draft_followup respond send_draft schedule_draft_send manage dm group channel room thread user server inbox draft

operation.description: Message subaction. One of: send, read_channel, read_with_contact, search, list_channels, list_servers, join, leave, react, edit, delete, pin, get_user, triage, list_inbox, search_inbox, draft_reply, draft_followup, respond, send_draft, schedule_draft_send, manage.

subaction.description: Alias for operation, accepted for planner compatibility.

subAction.description: Alias for operation, accepted for planner compatibility.

__subaction.description: Legacy alias for operation, accepted for planner compatibility.

op.description: Alias for operation, accepted for planner compatibility.

source.description: Connector source such as discord, slack, signal, whatsapp, telegram, x, imessage, matrix, line, google-chat, feishu, instagram, wechat, gmail.

accountId.description: Optional connector account id for multi-account message connectors.

sources.description: Optional inbox sources for op=triage, list_inbox, or search_inbox.

folder.description: Optional inbox folder hint for triage, list_inbox, search_inbox, draft, or respond operations.

target.description: Loose target reference: user, handle, channel, room, group, server, contact, phone, email, or platform-specific ID.

targetKind.description: Target kind for op=send: user, contact, channel, room, thread, group, server, email, or phone.

channel.description: Channel/room/group reference for read_channel, list_channels, join, leave.

roomId.description: Platform room or stored room ID.

server.description: Loose server, guild, workspace, or team name/reference.

serverId.description: Platform server, guild, workspace, or team ID.

userId.description: Platform user ID or stored entity ID.

entityId.description: Person/entity ID for read_with_contact, get_user, or scoped search.

platform.description: Optional platform filter for read_with_contact or search.

thread.description: Optional thread parent reference for op=send.

alias.description: Channel/room alias for op=join or op=leave.

invite.description: Invite URL or token for op=join.

message.description: Message text for op=send or replacement text for op=edit.

text.description: Replacement text for op=edit (alias of message).

query.description: Search term for op=search or op=search_inbox.

content.description: Inbox search text or message lookup hint for triage/draft/respond.

sender.description: Sender identifier for inbox search or reply lookup.

body.description: Draft or response body for draft_reply, draft_followup, respond.

reply.description: Alias for body when drafting or responding to a message.

replyText.description: Alias for body when drafting or responding to a message.

messageBody.description: Alias for body when drafting or responding to a message.

draftId.description: Draft identifier for op=send_draft or op=schedule_draft_send.

confirmed.description: Whether the user explicitly confirmed sending for op=send_draft.

messageId.description: Platform/full message ID or stored memory ID for react/edit/delete/pin/respond.

inReplyToId.description: Alias for messageId when drafting or responding to a message.

id.description: Alias for messageId, accepted for planner compatibility.

pin.description: Pin state for op=pin (false to unpin when supported).

manageOperation.description: Management operation for op=manage: archive, trash, spam, mark_read, label_add, label_remove, tag_add, tag_remove, mute_thread, unsubscribe.

label.description: Label for op=manage when adding/removing labels.

tag.description: Tag for op=manage when adding/removing tags.

urgency.description: Urgency for op=send: normal, important, urgent.

persist.description: Whether op=send persists outbound content to target room memory. Defaults true.

limit.description: Maximum number of items to return.

worldIds.description: Optional account/server scopes for inbox operations.

channelIds.description: Optional channel/conversation scopes for inbox operations.

until.description: End date/timestamp for op=read_channel range=dates or op=search_inbox.

function.name: PASSWORD_MANAGER
function.description: password manager 1Password|ProtonPass: search list inject_username inject_password (clipboard-only confirm-required no-plaintext-chat)

subaction.description: One of: search, list, inject_username, inject_password.

intent.description: Natural-language description of the lookup intent.

query.description: Search string matched against item title, URL, username, and tags.

itemId.description: Password manager item id (required for inject_* subactions).

field.description: Which field to inject when using a generic inject subaction. Ignored when subaction explicitly names the field.

confirmed.description: Must be explicitly true to copy a credential to the clipboard.

limit.description: Optional item limit for the `list` subaction (default 20).

function.name: PROFILE_SAVE
function.description: persist owner state: save(name,location,age,prefs) + capture_phone(number); reminder/escalation policy lives on LIFE.policy_*

subaction.description: Which profile operation to perform: save or capture_phone. The legacy `set` alias is canonicalized to `save`.

relationshipStatus.description: Relationship status such as single, partnered, married, or n/a.

age.description: Owner age or stable age descriptor when clearly stated.

travelBookingPreferences.description: Reusable flight and hotel preference checklist or summary.

key.description: Compatibility alias for generic profile set calls. Prefer explicit fields such as travelBookingPreferences.

value.description: Compatibility value for generic profile set calls; normalized into explicit owner profile fields when possible.

phoneNumber.description: Owner phone number for SMS/voice escalation routing.

allowSms.description: Allow SMS contact on the captured number.

allowVoice.description: Allow voice-call contact on the captured number.

function.name: REMOTE_DESKTOP
function.description: remote-desktop session lifecycle: start(confirmed,pairing-code) status(sessionId) end(sessionId) list revoke(sessionId)

subaction.description: One of: start, status, end, list, revoke.

sessionId.description: Session id — required for status, end, and revoke subactions.

confirmed.description: Must be true for start (security sensitive).

pairingCode.description: 6-digit one-time pairing code for start. Required unless ELIZA_REMOTE_LOCAL_MODE=1.

requesterIdentity.description: Identifier for who is asking (entity id, friend name, device id). Logged for audit on start.

intent.description: Freeform owner intent / reason for the session. Logged for audit.

function.name: SCHEDULED_TASK

kind.description: ScheduledTaskKind for create + filter for list. One of reminder, checkin, followup, approval, recap, watcher, output, custom.

status.description: Status filter for list (string or string[]). One of scheduled, fired, acknowledged, completed, skipped, expired, failed, dismissed.

subjectKind.description: ScheduledTaskSubject.kind: entity | relationship | thread | document | calendar_event | self.

ownerVisibleOnly.description: When true, list returns only ownerVisible tasks.

promptInstructions.description: create-only: prompt instructions stored on the task.

trigger.description: create-only: ScheduledTaskTrigger object (once / cron / interval / relative_to_anchor / during_window / event / manual / after_task).

respectsGlobalPause.description: create-only: when true, the task skips during global pause.

ownerVisible.description: create-only: when true, the task surfaces in owner views.

source.description: create-only: task source (default_pack | user_chat | first_run | plugin).

minutes.description: snooze-only: minutes to defer next fire.

untilIso.description: snooze-only: ISO-8601 timestamp to defer next fire to.

reason.description: skip / complete / dismiss / reopen: free-form reason recorded on the state log.

patch.description: update-only: shallow patch of editable ScheduledTask fields.

sinceIso.description: history-only: ISO-8601 lower bound on log occurredAtIso.

untilHistoryIso.description: history-only: ISO-8601 upper bound on log occurredAtIso.

includeRollups.description: history-only: include rolled-up daily summary log rows (default false; raw rows only).

function.name: SCHEDULING_NEGOTIATION
function.description: Multi-turn scheduling negotiation lifecycle: start, propose, respond, finalize, cancel, and list negotiations/proposals.

subaction.description: Which step of the negotiation to run: start, propose, respond, finalize, cancel, list_active, list_proposals.

intent.description: Free-text description of what the scheduling turn is trying to do.

negotiationId.description: Target negotiation ID for proposal, finalize, cancel, or list_proposals.

proposalId.description: Target proposal ID for respond or finalize.

subject.description: Subject of the meeting (used when starting a negotiation).

durationMinutes.description: Meeting duration in minutes (defaults to 30 when starting).

response.description: Proposal response: accepted, declined, or expired.

confirmed.description: Set true alongside a proposalId to finalize.

function.name: SCREEN_TIME
function.description: screen-time+activity+browser focus mins: summary today weekly weekly-avg-by-app by-app by-website activity-report time-on-app time-on-site browser-activity owner macOS

subaction.description: One of: summary, today, weekly, weekly_average_by_app, by_app, by_website, activity_report, time_on_app, time_on_site, browser_activity.

identifier.description: Specific app bundle id or website domain when filtering screen-time to one source.

days.description: Number of days back from now for weekly / weekly_average_by_app windows.

windowDays.description: Window in days for by_app / by_website summary queries.

windowHours.description: Window in hours for activity_report / time_on_app / time_on_site (default 24, max 720).

appNameOrBundleId.description: App name (e.g. 'Safari') or bundle id (e.g. 'com.apple.Safari') for time_on_app.

deviceId.description: Filter browser_activity to one registered device id; omit for default.

function.name: SUBSCRIPTIONS
function.description: paid sub audit+cancel via browser: audit | cancel(serviceSlug confirmed) | status(cancellationId); not email-list-unsubscribe

subaction.description: audit | cancel | status when supplied.

serviceName.description: Display name of the subscription service.

cancellationId.description: Ongoing cancellation id for status lookups.

queryWindowDays.description: Days of history for audit queries.

function.name: SUBSCRIPTIONS_AUDIT

function.name: TODO
function.description: todo manage list; op: write|create|update|complete|cancel|delete|list|clear; user-scoped (entityId).

op.description: Operation: write, create, update, complete, cancel, delete, list, clear.

todos.description: Array of {id?, content, status, activeForm?} for op=write. Replaces the user's list for this conversation.

includeCompleted.description: Include completed/cancelled todos in op=list output.

limit.description: Max rows to return for op=list.

function.name: TOGGLE_FEATURE
function.description: toggle LifeOps feature flight-booking push-notifs browser-automation escalation: enable | disable; registry-driven feature key set

featureKey.description: LifeOps feature key. Must be a key that has been registered in the FeatureFlagRegistry (see GET /api/lifeops/dev/registries → featureFlags for the live list).

enabled.description: True to enable, false to disable.

reason.description: Optional short reason captured for the audit log.

function.name: VOICE_CALL
function.description: Twilio voice dial: recipientKind=owner|external|e164; draft-confirm; approval-queue

subaction.description: Single canonical verb: `dial`.

recipientKind.description: Recipient discriminator: `owner` (escalation; uses owner number env), `external` (third party; recipient name resolved via relationships, allow-list checked), or `e164` (raw E.164 phone in `phoneNumber`).

phoneNumber.description: For recipientKind=e164: destination phone number in E.164 format (e.g. +15551234567).

recipient.description: For recipientKind=external: contact name or E.164 phone number. Names resolve via the relationships store.

bodyText.description: Optional spoken message played when the call connects.

confirmed.description: Must be true to actually place the call. Without it the action returns a draft / approval-queue entry.

reason.description: Optional reason describing why the call is being placed (recorded with the approval task).

function.name: WEBSITE_BLOCK
function.description: site block hosts-file: block(hosts,duration,confirm) unblock status request-permission release(ruleId,confirmed) list_active; macOS draft-then-confirm

subaction.description: One of: block, unblock, status, request_permission, release, list_active. When omitted the resolver extracts it from intent + recent conversation.

intent.description: Free-form description of what the owner wants. Required by the block subaction so heuristic + LLM extractors can pull hostnames and duration out.

hostnames.description: Public hostnames or URLs to block for the block subaction, e.g. ['x.com','twitter.com'].

durationMinutes.description: How long to block, in minutes. Omit for a manual block that stays active until unblocked. Null for indefinite.

confirmed.description: Set true only when the owner has explicitly confirmed the block. Without it, block returns a draft confirmation request. Also required by the release subaction.

ruleId.description: ID of the managed block rule to release. Required by the release subaction.

reason.description: Optional reason recorded on the rule when released. Used by the release subaction.

includeLiveStatus.description: Whether to include the current hosts-file/SelfControl live block state in the list_active response. Default true.

includeManagedRules.description: Whether to include managed LifeOps block rules in the list_active response. Default true.
